error.signature: org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.OffsetOutOfRangeException: Fetch offset 12345 is out of range for partition my_topic-0
error.regex: (OffsetOutOfRangeException|offset.*out of range|fetch offset.*out of range|log start offset.*greater than|OFFSET_OUT_OF_RANGE)

verdict.summary: Consumer's committed offset no longer exists in broker log. Log was truncated by retention policy or compaction.

dead_ends.action: Disable log retention to prevent future offset loss
dead_ends.why_fails: Unbounded retention fills disk and eventually crashes the broker. Use retention policies appropriate for consumer lag.

dead_ends.action: Set auto.offset.reset=none to surface the error
dead_ends.why_fails: With 'none', the consumer throws an exception and stops entirely instead of recovering. Only useful for exactly-once pipelines.

workarounds.action: Set auto.offset.reset=earliest or latest based on use case
workarounds.how: auto.offset.reset=earliest  # reprocess from beginning; or 'latest' to skip to current

workarounds.action: Monitor consumer lag to catch falling behind before offsets expire
workarounds.how: kafka-consumer-groups.sh --describe --group my_group --bootstrap-server localhost:9092  # check LAG column

workarounds.action: Increase log retention to exceed maximum expected consumer downtime
workarounds.how: log.retention.hours=168  # 7 days; must be longer than longest consumer outage
